---
title: "Teaching"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-header}
::: {.page-shell}
<div class="eyebrow">Teaching</div>

# Teaching and supervision

Undergraduate teaching in health economics and behavioural economics, alongside doctoral and research supervision in applied economics and health policy.
:::
:::

::: {.prose}
## Current courses

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.feature-card}
### ECON2112 · Health Economics and Finance

Economic analysis of health, healthcare markets, insurance, provider behaviour, economic evaluation and health-policy design.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### ECON3111 · Behavioural Economics

Decision-making under risk and uncertainty, intertemporal choice, social preferences, behavioural public policy and experimental evidence.
:::
:::

## Additional teaching

**ECON3011 · Health Economics and Finance**

Course coordination and teaching in applied health economics, economic evaluation and health-system decision-making.

## Research supervision

Current supervision includes doctoral research on telehealth use and costs, climate change and agriculture, and the economic evaluation of crop drought-resilience strategies. Previous supervision includes research on health-system reform and the socioeconomic consequences of vaccine mandates.
:::
